ogTitle: Millers Bluff vs Moina: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Millers Bluff and Moina.
heading: Millers Bluff vs Moina — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Millers Bluff, Tasmania and Moina, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Moina has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Millers Bluff (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Millers Bluff or Moina safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Millers Bluff has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Moina scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Millers Bluff scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. Moina scores 20/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Millers Bluff or Moina?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Moina has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.83% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
